plan: Create a `Tier` type union in `src/domain/card.ts` with literal values `"Open" | "Working" | "Deep"`
Create a `Category` type union in `src/domain/card.ts` with literal values `"Infrastructure" | "Working Together" | "Growth and Direction" | "Feedback and Repair"`

Create a `Card` interface in `src/domain/card.ts` with fields: `cardNumber: number`, `category: Category`, `tier: Tier`, `prompt: string`, `guidance: string`, `flavourText: string`, `pairsWith?: number`
Update `src/domain/index.ts` barrel to re-export everything from `./card`
Run `npm run typecheck` to verify TypeScript compilation succeeds
Run `npm run lint` to verify no lint errors
Run `npm run test` to verify existing tests still pass
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run typecheck` — confirms strict TypeScript compilation with the new types
Run `npm run lint` — confirms no lint violations (unused exports, etc.)
Run `npm run test` — confirms existing unit tests (folder-structure, project-setup, quality-scripts) still pass
No new unit tests needed for pure type definitions — types are validated at compile time
risks: tsconfig.app.json has `erasableSyntaxOnly: true` — must use `type` keyword for type exports (e.g., `export type { Card }`) rather than runtime constructs like `enum`. Use union types instead of enums.
tsconfig.app.json has `verbatimModuleSyntax: true` — all type-only re-exports from the barrel must use `export type` syntax
tsconfig.app.json enforces `noUnusedLocals` — types must be exported, not left as internal-only declarations
CSV column is `Prompt` not `Question` — the Card interface field should be `prompt` (matching actual CSV), not `question` (which the CLAUDE.md mentions as a possible field name)
